name: Brownie and Ice Cream Sandwiches with Chocolate
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 quarts (about 6 cups) vanilla ice cream, softened slightly
8 ounces bittersweet or semisweet chocolate, chopped
1 1/2 teaspoons instant espresso powder, dissolved in
recipeInstructions:
For Brownies:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C).
2. Line a 17-1/4 x 11-1/2 x 1-inch baking sheet with parchment paper.
3. In a heavy large saucepan, bring sugar, butter, and water to boil over medium heat, stirring until sugar dissolves. Reduce heat to low. Add chocolate; stir until smooth.
4. Whisk eggs and vanilla in a large bowl to blend. Gradually whisk in hot chocolate mixture.
5. Mix in flour, baking soda, and salt, then nuts. Spread batter over prepared sheet.
6. Bake until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, about 20 minutes. Cool completely.
7. Freeze until firm. Cut around brownies to loosen. Turn out onto a work surface. Peel off parchment.
8. Trim brownies to a 16 x 10-inch rectangle; cut in half crosswise, making two 8 x 10-inch rectangles.
9. Place one rectangle on the baking sheet. Cover with ice cream. Freeze until slightly firm.
10. Score the top of the remaining brownie in half lengthwise, then score across in 2-inch sections, marking 10 2 x 4-inch rectangles.
11. Press scored brownies atop the ice cream, scored side up. Freeze until ice cream is firm, at least 4 hours.
12. Cut brownies along marked lines into sandwiches.
For Sauce:
1. Stir chocolate and coffee in a saucepan over low heat until smooth. Add liqueur. Serve sandwiches with sauce.
